vllm.title: [CI/Build] Update torch to 2.4
vllm.author: SageMoore
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR updates pytorch to version 2.4. All dependencies that depend on pytorch have also been updated.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix RMSNorm forward in InternViT attention qk_layernorm
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FILL IN THE PR DESCRIPTION HERE
FIX #6977 (link existing issues this PR will resolve)

The use of fused RMSNorm in InternAttention causes serious numeric divergence, which may result the image hallucination mentioned in above issue.
This PR fixes the case by falling back to native_forward for RMSNor

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Remove sparseml requirement from testing
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We have coverage for compressed-tensors checkpoints with lm-eval-harness, so there is no need to keep this test and dependency around.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Lower gemma's unloaded_params exception to warning
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #6957
Sometimes we add extra tensors when constructing quantized models in order to cover all permutations of checkpoints. In the linked issue, Gemma currently fails when loading FP8 quantization because we add k/v_scale parameters to be loaded in the case that calibrated kv cache scales are ava

vllm.title: [Misc] Support attention logits soft-capping with flash-attn
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for attention logits soft-capping in the FlashAttention backend.
This is done by

Moving logits_soft_cap from AttentionMetadata to AttentionImpl.
Using vllm-flash-attn == 2.6.1 which added support for soft-capping.

Note that vllm-flash-attn v2.6.1 uses PyTorch 2.4.0, so the PR

vllm.title: [CI/Build][Bugfix] Fix CUTLASS header-only line
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
vLLM is currently building a lot of CUTLASS object files for no reason.
CUTLASS_ENABLE_HEADERS_ONLY wasn't being picked up by the CUTLASS build systems, as it was set as a variable rather than a cache variable.
I noticed this because my compile_commands.json is huge and full of cutlass object file b

vllm.title: [Performance] Optimize `get_seqs`
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR optimizes the overhead of seq_group.get_seqs(), which was reported by @youkaichao.
The solution is simple: We maintain seqs: List[Sequence] in addition to seqs_dict: Dict[int, Sequence], and use seqs for all get_seqs calls.
This leads to small performance boost (llama3 8B, 1xH100)

Before: T

vllm.title: [Kernel] Fix input for flashinfer prefill wrapper.
vllm.author: LiuXiaoxuanPKU

vllm.title: [mypy] Speed up mypy checking
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Turns out that placing the bare mypy command at the start instead of at the end can save around 30 seconds of time.
Since this is still too slow, I've also reinstated --follow-imports=skip for all files when run locally. The stricter checks are still being run in CI.
